availableCommands.name: debug
availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues (bundled)

availableCommands.name: simplify
availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: batch
availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: loop
availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) (bundled)

availableCommands.name: claude-api
availableCommands.description:
Build apps with the Claude API or Anthropic SDK.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`/`claude_agent_sdk`, or user asks to use Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Agent SDK.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: capturing-ideas
availableCommands.description: Use when tangential ideas, future features, or "we should also..." suggestions come up during focused work, OR when you see <idea-detection> context from the hook - captures ideas to a backlog file so they aren't lost while staying focused on current task (user)

availableCommands.name: superpowers:brainstorm
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Interactive design refinement using Socratic method

availableCommands.name: superpowers:write-plan
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Create detailed implementation plan with bite-sized tasks

availableCommands.name: superpowers:execute-plan
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Execute plan in batches with review checkpoints

availableCommands.name: search-conversations
availableCommands.description: (episodic-memory) Search previous Claude Code conversations using semantic or text search

availableCommands.name: superpowers:brainstorm
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Deprecated - use the superpowers:brainstorming skill instead

availableCommands.name: superpowers:execute-plan
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Deprecated - use the superpowers:executing-plans skill instead

availableCommands.name: superpowers:write-plan
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Deprecated - use the superpowers:writing-plans skill instead

availableCommands.name: defense-in-depth
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when invalid data causes failures deep in execution, requiring validation at multiple system layers - validates at every layer data passes through to make bugs structurally impossible

availableCommands.name: brainstorming
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when creating or developing, before writing code or implementation plans - refines rough ideas into fully-formed designs through collaborative questioning, alternative exploration, and incremental validation. Don't use during clear 'mechanical' processes

availableCommands.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when facing 3+ independent failures that can be investigated without shared state or dependencies - dispatches multiple Claude agents to investigate and fix independent problems concurrently

availableCommands.name: condition-based-waiting
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when tests have race conditions, timing dependencies, or inconsistent pass/fail behavior - replaces arbitrary timeouts with condition polling to wait for actual state changes, eliminating flaky tests from timing guesses

availableCommands.name: executing-plans
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when partner provides a complete implementation plan to execute in controlled batches with review checkpoints - loads plan, reviews critically, executes tasks in batches, reports for review between batches

availableCommands.name: finishing-a-development-branch
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

availableCommands.name: receiving-code-review
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

availableCommands.name: requesting-code-review
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements - dispatches superpowers:code-reviewer subagent to review implementation against plan or requirements before proceeding

availableCommands.name: root-cause-tracing
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when errors occur deep in execution and you need to trace back to find the original trigger - systematically traces bugs backward through call stack, adding instrumentation when needed, to identify source of invalid data or incorrect behavior

availableCommands.name: subagent-driven-development
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session - dispatches fresh subagent for each task with code review between tasks, enabling fast iteration with quality gates

availableCommands.name: systematic-debugging
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes - four-phase framework (root cause investigation, pattern analysis, hypothesis testing, implementation) that ensures understanding before attempting solutions

availableCommands.name: sharing-skills
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when you've developed a broadly useful skill and want to contribute it upstream via pull request - guides process of branching, committing, pushing, and creating PR to contribute skills back to upstream repository

availableCommands.name: testing-anti-patterns
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when writing or changing tests, adding mocks, or tempted to add test-only methods to production code - prevents testing mock behavior, production pollution with test-only methods, and mocking without understanding dependencies

availableCommands.name: test-driven-development
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code - write the test first, watch it fail, write minimal code to pass; ensures tests actually verify behavior by requiring failure first

availableCommands.name: testing-skills-with-subagents
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when creating or editing skills, before deployment, to verify they work under pressure and resist rationalization - applies RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle to process documentation by running baseline without skill, writing to address failures, iterating to close loopholes

availableCommands.name: using-git-worktrees
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification

availableCommands.name: using-superpowers
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when starting any conversation - establishes mandatory workflows for finding and using skills, including using Skill tool before announcing usage, following brainstorming before coding, and creating TodoWrite todos for checklists

availableCommands.name: verification-before-completion
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always

availableCommands.name: writing-plans
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when design is complete and you need detailed implementation tasks for engineers with zero codebase context - creates comprehensive implementation plans with exact file paths, complete code examples, and verification steps assuming engineer has minimal domain knowledge

availableCommands.name: writing-skills
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment - applies TDD to process documentation by testing with subagents before writing, iterating until bulletproof against rationalization

availableCommands.name: remembering-conversations
availableCommands.description: (episodic-memory) Use when user asks 'how should I...' or 'what's the best approach...' after exploring code, OR when you've tried to solve something and are stuck, OR for unfamiliar workflows, OR when user references past work. Searches conversation history.

availableCommands.name: frontend-design
availableCommands.description: (frontend-design) Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.

availableCommands.name: brainstorming
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

availableCommands.name: executing-plans
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

availableCommands.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

availableCommands.name: finishing-a-development-branch
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

availableCommands.name: subagent-driven-development
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

availableCommands.name: requesting-code-review
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

availableCommands.name: receiving-code-review
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

availableCommands.name: systematic-debugging
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

availableCommands.name: using-superpowers
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions

availableCommands.name: writing-plans
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

availableCommands.name: verification-before-completion
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always

availableCommands.name: test-driven-development
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

availableCommands.name: writing-skills
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

availableCommands.name: using-git-worktrees
availableCommands.description: (superpowers) Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification

availableCommands.name: compact
availableCommands.description: Clear conversation history but keep a summary in context. Optional: /compact [instructions for summarization]
